passages.label: Text Message
passages.text:
From: Marcus Chen
To: Priya Patel

The building manager just confirmed the elevator will be out of service this Friday from 8 a.m. to 3 p.m. for its annual inspection. Since we're on the 6th floor, could you let the delivery company know to reschedule our furniture order for a different day? I'll be in meetings all morning and can't make the call myself. Thanks!

questions.text: Why did Marcus send this message to Priya?
options: To ask her to reschedule a delivery
To report an elevator malfunction
To invite her to a building inspection
To request furniture recommendations
questions.explanation: Marcus explains the elevator situation and directly asks Priya to contact the delivery company to reschedule — that is the main purpose of the message.

questions.text: What is the reason Marcus cannot make the phone call himself?
options: He does not have the delivery company's number
He will be busy in meetings
He is not in the office that day
He is waiting for the building manager
questions.explanation: Marcus states he'll be 'in meetings all morning and can't make the call myself.'

passages.label: Official Notice
passages.text:
Subject: Annual Campus Fitness Center Membership Renewal

Dear Student,

Welcome back to the new semester! This is a reminder that your Fitness Center membership expired on August 31. To continue using the facility, you must renew your membership by September 15 to avoid the late renewal fee of $25.

Renewal can be completed online through the Student Services Portal using your university login. You may choose from three membership tiers: Basic (gym access only), Standard (gym + group classes), or Premium (gym + classes + personal training sessions). Payment must be made in full at the time of renewal.

Once renewed, your membership card will be reactivated within 24 hours. Please note that the Fitness Center will be closed for maintenance on September 10 and 11. If you have any questions, contact the Student Services office at services@university.edu.

Warm regards,
Student Services Office

questions.text: What is the main purpose of this notice?
options: To announce the opening of a new fitness center
To remind students to renew their membership
To inform students of a fee increase
To describe new fitness classes available this semester
questions.explanation: The notice is primarily a reminder about membership renewal, including the deadline and the process to renew.

questions.text: What will happen if a student renews after September 15?
options: Their membership will be permanently cancelled
They will need to visit the office in person
They will be charged an additional $25
Their access will be limited to gym only
questions.explanation: The notice states students must renew by September 15 'to avoid the late renewal fee of $25.'

questions.text: Which membership tier would a student choose if they want group classes but NOT personal training?
options: Basic
Standard
Premium
Any tier includes group classes
questions.explanation: Standard includes 'gym + group classes' but not personal training, which is only in Premium.

questions.text: When will a renewed membership card be usable again?
options: Immediately after payment
After September 15
Within 24 hours of renewal
On the first day of the new month
questions.explanation: The notice says 'your membership card will be reactivated within 24 hours' after renewal.

passages.label: Email
passages.text:
From: Jordan Rivera
To: Neighborhood Watch Group

Hello everyone,

I wanted to give a quick update following last week's community safety meeting. As discussed, we have agreed to launch a neighborhood watch schedule starting October 1. Volunteers will walk the main streets in pairs every Tuesday and Thursday evening from 7 to 9 p.m.

To sign up, please reply to this email with your preferred dates for October. Try to select at least two evenings. We have a total of eight slots to fill each month, and we want to make sure no single person is overloaded.

For those who could not attend the meeting, a summary of the new reporting procedures has been attached to this email. Please read it before your first patrol. All incidents should now be reported via the city's new app rather than by calling the non-emergency line.

Thank you for your continued support in keeping our neighborhood safe.

Best,
Jordan Rivera
Neighborhood Watch Coordinator

questions.text: What is Jordan asking recipients to do?
options: Attend a community safety meeting
Download the city's new app
Reply with their availability for patrols
Read the reporting procedures and call the non-emergency line
questions.explanation: Jordan asks everyone to 'reply to this email with your preferred dates for October' — that is the main action requested.

questions.text: How should incidents be reported according to the new procedures?
options: By calling the non-emergency police line
By contacting Jordan Rivera directly
Through the city's new app
By attending the next community meeting
questions.explanation: Jordan states 'All incidents should now be reported via the city's new app rather than by calling the non-emergency line.'

questions.text: What does Jordan want to avoid by limiting each person to at least two evenings?
options: Having the same people patrol too frequently
Leaving some slots empty
Overloading individual volunteers
Conflicts between patrol pairs
questions.explanation: Jordan says 'we want to make sure no single person is overloaded,' which is why participation is managed.

passages.label: Text Message
passages.text:
From: Dr. Chen
To: Study Group

Reminder that our weekly study session has been moved from Thursday to Friday this week due to the lab being reserved for a departmental event. Same time, 6 PM, same room. Please let anyone who isn't in this group chat know. See you then!

questions.text: Why is the study session being moved?
options: The room will be used for another event
Dr. Chen has a conflict on Thursday
The group voted to change the day
Friday is more convenient for everyone
questions.explanation: Dr. Chen states the lab is "reserved for a departmental event" — that is the reason for the change.

questions.text: What does Dr. Chen ask the recipients to do?
options: Arrive early to reserve the room
Confirm their attendance by Thursday
Inform others not in the group chat
Reschedule the session to a different week
questions.explanation: Dr. Chen asks them to "let anyone who isn't in this group chat know" about the change.

passages.label: Email
passages.text:
Subject: Library Printing Services — Temporary Outage

Dear Library Users,

Please be advised that the library's printing services will be unavailable from Monday, May 5 through Wednesday, May 7 while our printing system undergoes a scheduled upgrade. During this period, students who need to print documents urgently are encouraged to use the printing kiosks located in the Student Union Building on the ground floor.

Once the upgrade is complete, users will benefit from faster print speeds, improved wireless printing from personal devices, and a new credit-based payment system that replaces the current card system. All existing print credits will be transferred automatically.

We apologize for any inconvenience this may cause. If you have any questions, please contact the library help desk at library@university.edu.

Library Services Team

questions.text: What is the main purpose of this email?
options: To announce the permanent closure of library printing
To inform users about a temporary printing outage
To introduce a new student payment system
To advertise the Student Union Building services
questions.explanation: The email's primary purpose is to inform users that printing will be unavailable from May 5–7 due to a scheduled upgrade.

questions.text: Where can students print during the outage?
options: The main library reference desk
Any computer lab on campus
The Student Union Building kiosks
The faculty resource center
questions.explanation: The email directs students to "the printing kiosks located in the Student Union Building on the ground floor."

questions.text: What will happen to existing print credits after the upgrade?
options: They will expire and need to be repurchased
They will be transferred automatically
Students must redeem them before the upgrade
They will be converted to library fines credit
questions.explanation: The email states "All existing print credits will be transferred automatically."

passages.label: Flyer
passages.text:
INTERNATIONAL FOOD FESTIVAL — CAMPUS GREEN
Saturday, April 19 | 11 AM – 5 PM

Join us for the 8th Annual International Food Festival, celebrating cuisines from over 30 countries! This free, family-friendly event features:

• Live cooking demonstrations at 12 PM and 3 PM
• Cultural performances throughout the day
• Food stalls run by student cultural associations
• A children's craft zone (ages 4–12)
• Raffle prizes including airline vouchers and restaurant gift cards

All food purchases support student cultural association programming. No tickets required — just show up and enjoy!

For more information or to volunteer, visit: campus.edu/foodfestival

questions.text: What is one way the event supports the university community?
options: It provides free meals to all students
Food purchases fund student cultural programs
It distributes airline vouchers to attendees
It offers cooking classes for academic credit
questions.explanation: The flyer states "All food purchases support student cultural association programming."

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT mentioned as a feature of the festival?
options: Live cooking demonstrations
A children's craft zone
A photography competition
Cultural performances
questions.explanation: The flyer lists demonstrations, a craft zone, and performances, but does not mention a photography competition.

questions.text: How can someone participate as a volunteer?
options: By emailing the cultural associations directly
By visiting the event website
By arriving early on the day of the event
By contacting the student services office
questions.explanation: The flyer says "to volunteer, visit: campus.edu/foodfestival" — the website is the contact point.

passages.label: Notice
passages.text:
PARKING NOTICE
All vehicles parked in Lot C must be removed by 7 AM on Saturday, March 22. The lot will be closed for resurfacing and will reopen Monday morning. Vehicles remaining after 7 AM will be towed at the owner's expense. For temporary parking, use the overflow lot on Pine Street (free on weekends).
— Campus Facilities Management

questions.text: What will happen to cars left in Lot C after 7 AM on Saturday?
options: They will be ticketed but not moved
They will be towed at the owner's cost
They will be relocated to Pine Street
The deadline will be extended to Monday
questions.explanation: The notice clearly states "Vehicles remaining after 7 AM will be towed at the owner's expense."

questions.text: What does the notice say about Pine Street parking on weekends?
options: It requires a parking permit
It is free of charge
It is available for staff only
It closes at 5 PM
questions.explanation: The notice specifies the Pine Street overflow lot is "free on weekends."

passages.label: Email
passages.text:
From: Academic Advising Office
To: All Undergraduate Students
Subject: Course Registration — Important Reminders

Dear Students,

As the spring registration period approaches, we would like to remind you of several important policies.

First, students must meet with their academic advisor before registering if they plan to take more than 18 credit hours in a single semester. An advisor approval form must be submitted to the registrar's office no later than November 1.

Second, students who have outstanding library fines or unpaid tuition balances will have a registration hold placed on their accounts. These holds must be cleared before registration can proceed.

Third, please review your degree audit in the student portal before selecting courses to ensure you are meeting all graduation requirements. The advising office is available Monday through Friday, 9 AM to 4 PM, for walk-in appointments or scheduled consultations.

We look forward to helping you plan a successful semester.

Academic Advising Office

questions.text: Who must meet with an advisor before registering?
options: All undergraduate students
Students taking more than 18 credit hours
Students with unpaid library fines
First-year students only
questions.explanation: The email specifies that students "who plan to take more than 18 credit hours" must meet with an advisor before registering.

questions.text: What prevents a student from registering for courses?
options: Not having declared a major
Missing the November 1 deadline
Having a hold due to unpaid fines or tuition
Failing to attend an orientation session
questions.explanation: The email states that "outstanding library fines or unpaid tuition balances will have a registration hold placed on their accounts."

questions.text: What does the advising office recommend students do before choosing courses?
options: Contact their professors for permission
Review their degree audit in the student portal
Submit a course preference form online
Attend an advising workshop
questions.explanation: The email advises students to "review your degree audit in the student portal before selecting courses."

questions.text: When is the advising office available for walk-in appointments?
options: Monday through Saturday, 8 AM to 5 PM
Monday through Friday, 9 AM to 4 PM
Weekdays only, by appointment only
Tuesday and Thursday, 10 AM to 3 PM
questions.explanation: The email states the office is available "Monday through Friday, 9 AM to 4 PM, for walk-in appointments or scheduled consultations."

passages.label: Text Message
passages.text:
From: Maya
To: Lena

Hey! The professor just posted an update on the class portal — the essay deadline has been extended to next Friday because she said a lot of people had questions about the rubric. She's also added a FAQ document to the resources folder. You might want to read it before you start writing.

questions.text: Why was the essay deadline extended?
options: The professor was ill and unavailable
Many students were confused about the rubric
The class portal had technical issues
Students requested more research time
questions.explanation: Maya says the deadline was extended "because she said a lot of people had questions about the rubric."

questions.text: What does Maya suggest Lena do?
options: Contact the professor directly
Read the FAQ document before writing
Submit a draft for early feedback
Check the portal for the new deadline
questions.explanation: Maya tells Lena "You might want to read it before you start writing," referring to the FAQ document.

passages.label: Official Notice
passages.text:
CITY OF RIVERSIDE
COMMUNITY RECYCLING PROGRAM — UPDATED GUIDELINES

Effective July 1, the City of Riverside is expanding its recycling program to include glass containers and rigid plastics marked with recycling codes 1 through 7. Please note the following changes:

• Glass bottles and jars should be rinsed and placed in the blue recycling bin WITHOUT lids.
• Rigid plastic containers (e.g., yogurt tubs, laundry detergent bottles) are now accepted.
• Plastic bags and soft plastic wrapping are NOT accepted in curbside bins. Please take these to drop-off locations at participating grocery stores.
• Paper and cardboard should continue to be flattened and placed in the bin separately from glass and plastic.

Contaminated recyclables (items with food residue) will cause entire loads to be sent to landfill. Please rinse all containers before recycling.

For a full list of accepted materials, visit riverside.gov/recycling.

questions.text: What is new about the recycling program as of July 1?
options: Paper recycling is no longer accepted
Glass and certain plastics are now included
All plastic bags can now be recycled curbside
The recycling bin color has been changed
questions.explanation: The notice states the program is "expanding to include glass containers and rigid plastics" — these are the new additions.

questions.text: What should residents do with plastic bags?
options: Place them in the blue recycling bin
Drop them off at participating grocery stores
Seal them in paper bags before recycling
Include them with cardboard items
questions.explanation: The notice says plastic bags "are NOT accepted in curbside bins" and should be taken to "drop-off locations at participating grocery stores."

questions.text: Why might recyclables end up in a landfill?
options: If they are placed in the wrong bin color
If they contain food residue
If glass lids are left on jars
If plastics are mixed with paper
questions.explanation: The notice warns that "contaminated recyclables (items with food residue) will cause entire loads to be sent to landfill."

tips: Scan the whole document quickly before reading questions — these are short!
Identify the purpose of the document: Why was this email/notice written?
Pay attention to who is doing what — keep roles and responsibilities clear.
Watch for tricky distractors that use words from the text but give wrong answers.
Make simple inferences — not everything is stated directly.
Answer quickly and move on; don't overthink short passages.
whatToExpect: Content includes emails, notices, text messages, pamphlets, invoices.
Topics involve everyday campus or social situations.
Questions ask about main purpose, specific details, and simple inferences.
